commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an in-flight coalescer for Codex dynamic tool calls and wires it into the app-server attempt and side-question paths with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +67, Tests +138. Total +205 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main handles each matching item/tool/call independently, and the PR tests/proof model two concurrent same-thread, same-turn requests with different callIds and identical semantic arguments. I did not run the repro locally because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review of the narrow runtime fix and decision to land or keep the draft paused.

target.title: fix(codex): coalesce in-flight dynamic tool calls
target.body:
## Summary

What changed?
- Add a Codex dynamic-tool in-flight coalescer keyed by `threadId`, `turnId`, `namespace`, `tool`, and canonicalized `arguments`.
- Wire the coalescer into both the main Codex app-server run path and the `/btw` side-question dynamic-tool path.
- Add regression coverage for stable duplicate keys, in-flight-only coalescing, and side-thread duplicate request handling.

## Why

Codex app-server can issue two `item/tool/call` requests with different `callId`s but identical tool name and arguments while the first call is still running. Because OpenClaw previously tracked execution by `callId`, both requests reached the underlying OpenClaw tool execution path. For side-effecting tools, that can duplicate external effects such as sending the same screenshot or message twice.

This change only joins duplicate calls that are still in flight within the same turn. It does not persist a dedupe cache and does not suppress later sequential repeats, so intentional repeated tool use after the first call settles still works.

## Linked context

Related issue: #80040

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Duplicate Codex app-server dynamic-tool requests in the same thread and turn with different `callId`s but identical tool name and arguments now share the first in-flight execution instead of invoking the underlying OpenClaw tool twice.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS 15.6, Node v24.13.1, branch `codex/dedupe-inflight-dynamic-tools`, commit `3af143b53f17`. The probe used the real `createCodexDynamicToolBridge`, `createCodexDynamicToolInFlightCoalescer`, and `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` runtime path with a side-effecting OpenClaw dynamic tool that appends to a temp log file. Channel tokens and live message targets were not used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` local runtime probe that created one side-effecting OpenClaw dynamic tool, then concurrently submitted two Codex `item/tool/call`-equivalent requests with `callId=call-send-a` and `callId=call-send-b`, same `threadId=proof-thread-2026-05-30`, same `turnId=proof-turn-1`, same tool, and semantically identical arguments with object keys in different order.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local runtime probe:

```text
OpenClaw Codex dynamic tool in-flight proof
source branch: codex/dedupe-inflight-dynamic-tools
threadId: proof-thread-2026-05-30
turnId: proof-turn-1
request A callId: call-send-a
request B callId: call-send-b
semantic args equal after canonicalization: yes
underlying tool execute count: 1
side-effect log line count: 1
side-effect log:
2026-05-30T02:43:20.065Z callId=call-send-a text=PowerBI daily report ready target=redacted-feishu-channel
first response: {"contentItems":[{"type":"inputText","text":"sent:PowerBI daily report ready"}],"success":true}
duplicate response: {"contentItems":[{"type":"inputText","text":"sent:PowerBI daily report ready"}],"success":true}
same response object: true
```

- Observed result after fix: The first in-flight request stored the execution promise. The duplicate request with the same semantic key joined that promise, the underlying side-effecting tool executed once, the temp side-effect log received one line, and both Codex dynamic-tool responses resolved to the same successful response object.
- What was not tested: No live Feishu, PowerBI, or Gateway channel message was sent from this PR branch. The proof covers the narrow same-turn in-flight duplicate dynamic-tool class. It does not close broader duplicate mechanisms in #80040 such as provider-switch or abort-overlap cleanup, trajectory writer scoping, or LCM double-write behavior.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`
- `scripts/pre-commit/run-node-tool.sh oxfmt --write --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`
- `git diff --check`
